title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 432: Daredevil: Born Again Season 2 Recap and Review
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts break down **Daredevil: Born Again Season 2, diving into the next chapter of Matt Murdock’s war for the soul of New York City. With tensions escalating around Wilson Fisk’s growing influence and Matt struggling to balance justice, faith, and vengeance, the season pushes every major player closer to the edge. The hosts unpack the returning characters, the street-level stakes, and how the series continues building on the legacy of the original Daredevil show while carving out its own identity within the MCU. From brutal action and legal drama to the emotional fallout surrounding Matt Murdock, this is a full breakdown of a season that raises the pressure on every front.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 431: Marvel / DC: Spider-Man  / Superman #1 (2026)
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts break down Superman/Spider-Man #1, the latest crossover bringing two icons back together in a way that feels both classic and modern. Written by Brad Meltzer with art by Pepe Larraz, the main story gets a full dissection as the hosts explore how this team captures the ideals, tone, and dynamic between Superman and Spider-Man while delivering a crossover that feels worthy of both legacies. The episode also digs into the issue’s backup stories, spotlighting contributions from Tom King and Mitch Gerads, as well as Rainbow Rowell and Humberto Ramos. Each creative team brings a distinct tone and perspective, from introspective character work to more energetic, classic superhero storytelling, expanding the scope of the crossover beyond the main event. It is a layered look at a crossover that is not just about two legends meeting again, but about how multiple creative voices interpret that moment in different ways.

title: Major Issues Podcast: Ep 430: Invincible Season 4 Recap and Review
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts break down Invincible Season 4, diving headfirst into the chaos of the Viltrumite War and everything it means for Mark Grayson’s future. They unpack the season’s biggest arcs, from the growing threat of Thragg and the Viltrumite Empire to the introduction of Dinosaurus, whose twisted logic forces Mark to confront what saving the world actually costs. The hosts dig into the moral compromises, the escalating violence, and the way this season pushes Invincible further into uncomfortable territory. From massive, universe-shaking conflict to deeply personal consequences, the discussion explores how Season 4 raises the stakes across the board and tests Mark in ways we have not seen before. This is a full breakdown of a season that refuses to pull its punches.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 428: Josie & The Pussycats (2001) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts celebrate 20 years of Josie and the Pussycats, the cult classic that went from misunderstood box office flop to one of the sharpest satires of the music industry and consumer culture. They revisit the film’s ahead-of-its-time commentary on branding, corporate influence, and manufactured fame, while breaking down the performances, soundtrack, and chaotic energy that helped it find a second life years later. The conversation also explores why the movie resonates more now than it did on release and how it carved out its own legacy. From its biting humor to its pop-punk attitude, this is a retrospective on a film that was always smarter than it got credit for. But does it hold up? Tune in and see!

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 427: DC/Marvel: Superman/ Spider-Man #1
embedCode:
This week on Major Issues, the guys dive into the massive 2026 crossover event Superman / Spider-Man #1. Mark Waid and Jorge Jiménez lead the charge with a story that finally pits the Man of Steel and the Web-Slayer against a Brainiac and Doc Ock alliance. The crew weighs in on whether the chemistry between Clark and Peter lives up to the hype and if Waid’s love for comic history makes this a modern classic or just a heavy dose of nostalgia. The episode also breaks down the dense lineup of backup stories and the high-profile teams behind them. They shout out the Lois and MJ spotlight by Tom King and Jim Lee, the chaotic Jimmy Olsen short by Matt Fraction and Steve Lieber, and Christopher Priest’s meta-story with Daniel Sampere. With additional contributions from creators like Sean Murphy, Jeff Lemire, Greg Rucka, and Gail Simone, the hosts debate if this oversized special justifies its price tag or if it’s just a flashy celebration of the Big Two playing nice again.

embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts revisit Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice ten years later to unpack one of the most divisive superhero films ever released. They break down Zack Snyder’s ambitious vision, the film’s heavy themes around power, fear, and godhood, and the controversial choices that sparked endless debate among fans. title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 425: Brett Bean Interview!
embedCode:
THE Brett Bean Pulls Up to Major Discourse ⚔️ We sit down with Brett Bean, the talented creator behind D'Orc, and get into what drives the work beyond the blood and steel. This one is about grit. Betting on yourself. Putting something raw on the page and standing on it. 🗡️ D’Orc: Where it came from, why it had to exist, and what Brett needed to channel into the story. 🔄 Starting Over: We talk about creative resets. Walking away. Rebuilding momentum. Finding your voice again when the industry shifts or when you do. 🌎 Staying Grounded: In a loud and divided space, how do you stay focused and let the art speak? Brett shares how he filters out distraction and keeps his priorities straight. 🧠 Ownership: The weight of building your own world. The pressure that comes with it. The responsibility of steering the vision. ⚡ The Spark: What set him on this creative journey into comics and how he keeps himself inspired. Guys, this is a conversation about discipline, perspective, and trusting your instincts when you decide to go all in, and we really hope you enjoy it.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 424: Annihilation (2006) 20th Anniversary Recap & Review
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts dive into Annihilation, the cosmic Marvel event that reshaped the publisher’s spacefaring universe. Written primarily by Keith Giffen, the story unleashes the devastating Annihilation Wave across the galaxy and forces unlikely heroes to rise in the face of total extinction.

The hosts break down the event’s massive scope, from Nova stepping into the spotlight to major roles played by characters like Drax the Destroyer, Silver Surfer, and Thanos. They also explore how Annihilation revitalized Marvel’s cosmic corner and laid the foundation for years of stories that followed.

If you love epic space opera, forgotten heroes getting their moment, and one of Marvel’s most beloved modern events, this is a deep dive you won’t want to miss.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 422: Deadpool 10 Year Anniversary Recap & Review
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts revisit Deadpool ten years later to ask the big question: did it age like fine wine or like warm chimichangas? They break down how the film reshaped superhero marketing, pushed the boundaries of R-rated comic book movies, and cemented Ryan Reynolds as the definitive Wade Wilson. From its impact on the genre to its place in the larger comic book movie boom of the 2010s, this is a retrospective on the little superhero film that bet on itself and changed the game.

title: Major Issues Ep 421: Major Discourse With Phillip Kennedy Johnson!
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the Clique sits down with Phillip Kennedy Johnson for a wide-ranging conversation about monsters, morality, fatherhood, and what it really means to carry a legacy. One of the most emotionally grounded and thematically ambitious voices in modern comics joins the show to unpack both his current work and the evolution of his storytelling philosophy.

Inside the episode, the discussion dives into his horror-driven work on The Incredible Hulk and Infernal Hulk, exploring the theological undercurrents and how this era reframes the monster within Bruce Banner. The conversation also turns to his sweeping run on Superman, from the Warworld saga to the ongoing effort to rebuild hope around the Man of Steel.

Phillip opens up about how fatherhood shapes his writing and why themes of legacy and emotional inheritance keep surfacing in his work. The hosts also dig into his approach to Batman and Robin, the challenge of balancing myth with intimacy, and what makes the Bruce and Damian dynamic so compelling right now. Plus, he gives a behind-the-scenes look at pitching Hellhunters and what it really takes to launch new ideas inside massive shared universes.

If you care about heart, horror, and heroes carrying impossible weight, this is one you do not want to miss.

heroImage.alt: Major Issues Ep 421: Major Discourse With Phillip Kennedy Johnson!

embedCode:

From its influence on modern comics and film to its lasting impact on Batman’s characterization, this is a deep dive into one of the most important graphic novels ever published.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 410: Wonder Woman Dead Earth
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts step into the wasteland to break down Wonder Woman: Dead Earth by Daniel Warren Johnson, one of the most visceral, emotional, and visually jaw-dropping Elseworlds tales DC has published in years. The crew explores how Johnson reimagines Diana as a mythic survivor in a ruined world, forced to confront the haunting truth behind humanity’s fall and her own role in it. We dig into the brutal action, the heavy metal energy of Johnson’s artwork, the heartbreaking character turns, and the way this story strips Wonder Woman down to her core ideals to see what still shines through. It is a raw, explosive, and deeply human take on the Amazon Princess, and we are unpacking every punch, revelation, and moment of heartbreak that makes Dead Earth an instant classic.

embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts dive into the chaos, brilliance, and pure comic-book weirdness of Grant Morrison’s Batman/Deadpool from DC/Marvel: Batman/Deadpool #1. Morrison throws the Dark Knight into Deadpool’s reality-bending orbit, and the results are exactly as wild as you’d expect. Tune in for a deep, energetic breakdown of one of the strangest—and most entertaining—team-ups in modern comics.

title: Ep 407: V for Vendetta: 20 Year Retrospective
embedCode:
This week on the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts don their Guy Fawkes masks and look back at V for Vendetta, 20 years after its premiere. What began as a cautionary tale about authoritarian control, surveillance, and manufactured fear feels eerily prophetic two decades later. The crew revisits the Wachowskis-produced classic, exploring how its themes of rebellion, censorship, and identity hit harder in today’s world than ever before. From Hugo Weaving’s chillingly poetic performance to Natalie Portman’s transformation as Evey Hammond, we reflect on how the film’s dystopian vision has crept frighteningly close to reality—and why V for Vendetta remains a timeless rallying cry for freedom and truth.

title: Major Issues Ep 401: Dissecting The Ultimates
embedCode:
On this episode of the Major Issues Podcast, we explore Jonathan Hickman and Stefano Caselli’s Ultimates, the crown jewel of Marvel’s new Ultimate Universe (Earth 6160). This is not your classic Avengers lineup. Iron Lad, Thor, Wasp, and Captain America come together under extraordinary circumstances, while the shadow of Reed Richards, now the infamous Doom, looms large over everything. We dig into how Hickman redefines Earth’s Mightiest Heroes, the bold political themes shaping their mission, and why Reed’s transformation into Doom makes him one of the most compelling threats in the entire Ultimate line. What sets these Ultimates apart? How does Doom’s presence change the game for the whole universe? We break it all down with curiosity, excitement, and a true love of superhero storytelling.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 396: Godzilla VS Recap and Review
embedCode:
On this week’s episode of the Major Issues Podcast, the hosts dig into Marvel’s epic six-part monster mash where Godzilla rampages through the Marvel Universe and every corner of the superhero world scrambles to stop him. From Spider-Man webbing up a skyscraper-sized foot, to the Fantastic Four testing the limits of their cosmic science, to the X-Men realizing even mutant powers aren’t enough, this event puts Earth’s Mightiest Heroes through the wringer. Along the way, Hulk and Thor step in for some truly seismic brawls, Fin Fang Foom rears his head as a rival “King of the Monsters,” and S.H.I.E.L.D. tries (and mostly fails) to keep order with Maria Hill calling the shots. We break down every outrageous showdown, every clash of egos, and why Jet Jaguar and the so-called Kaiju Chasers United might just be the weirdest allies Marvel’s ever introduced. If you’ve ever wondered what it looks like when superheroes, kaiju, and Marvel’s trademark chaos all collide, this series is it — and we’ve got all the monster-sized details.

embedCode:
Before Fantastic Four: First Steps arrives, the hosts of the Major Issues Podcast dive into two of the most essential stories in Fantastic Four history! With Sylvester Stallone behind the visor, this cult classic aimed to bring the 2000 AD icon to life—complete with oversized armor, dystopian chaos, and questionable comedy relief. But beyond the explosions and one-liners, the crew dives into the film’s deeper themes: authoritarianism, identity, and the cost of absolute justice. Was Judge Dredd ahead of its time, or just a stylish misfire with a killer helmet? Three decades later, we put the law on trial.

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 386: Batman Forever Recap and Review
embedCode:
In this episode, the hosts revisit the neon-soaked, over-the-top world of Batman Forever in honor of its 30th anniversary! From Val Kilmer’s stoic take on the Dark Knight to Jim Carrey’s unhinged Riddler and Tommy Lee Jones’ chaotic Two-Face, we explore what worked, what didn’t, and why this Joel Schumacher-directed spectacle remains one of the most divisive Batman films ever made. Was it a misunderstood gem or the beginning of the Bat-franchise’s descent into camp? Tune in as we dissect the film’s legacy, behind-the-scenes drama, and the tantalizing myth of the Schumacher Cut. Holy anniversary, Batman!

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 382: Dark Avengers #1-9 Recap and Review
embedCode:
What happens when the world’s greatest villains masquerade as its most beloved heroes? In this week’s episode of the Major Issues Podcast, the gang dives deep into the first 9 issues of Dark Avengers by Brian Michael Bendis and Mike Deodato Jr.! Spinning out of the events of Secret Invasion, Norman Osborn has been handed the keys to the kingdom—and he wastes no time assembling a twisted version of the Avengers filled with killers, manipulators, and madmen. We break down the deception, power plays, and psychological warfare that make this title a defining chapter of Marvel’s “Dark Reign” era. From Sentry’s instability to Moonstone’s manipulation, and Ares’ reluctant involvement to Osborn’s descent into madness—nothing is off-limits. Join us as we explore what happens when the line between hero and villain is completely shattered!

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 371: Major Discourse w/ Joe Kelly!
embedCode:
From passionate fans to voices in the industry, Comic Book Clique has grown into something bigger than we ever imagined. We've reviewed comics, analyzed stories, and shared our love for this medium—but now, we're taking things to the next level. MAJOR DISCOURSE is our newest video series, where we sit down with the most influential creators in comics to have the conversations that matter. No fluff—just real discussions about storytelling, creativity, and the industry itself. For our very first episode, we’re kicking things off with none other than Joe Kelly—the legendary writer behind Superman: Action Comics, Deadpool, I Kill Giants, and more. His impact on comics is undeniable, and we couldn’t think of a better guest to start this journey. This is a milestone for us, and we want you to be part of it. title: Major Issues Ep 356: The Penguin S1 Recap and Review
embedCode:
In this episode of The Major Issues Podcast, we dive into the latest addition to the Batman universe, The Penguin—a gritty spinoff from Matt Reeves' The Batman film. Set in the dark and twisted Gotham City, this series takes us deeper into the rise of Oz Cobb (Colin Farrell) as he claws his way to power in the criminal underworld. We break down the show’s strengths, from its razor-sharp character development to its moody, crime-filled atmosphere, and explore how it expands on the world established in The Batman. Whether you're a fan of Reeves' Batman or just love a good Gotham tale, this episode is packed with insights and analysis you won’t want to miss!

With the film promising a whole host of Deadpool variants across the vast multiverse, there is no better time for us to dive into the mind-bending world of Deadpool Kills Deadpool where The Merc With The Mouth faces off against... himself! Grab your chimichangas and get ready for a podcast filled with laughs, insights, and maybe a few existential crises along the way!

title: Major Issues 336: DC's Identity Crisis #1-7 (2004) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Mind manipulation. Murder. Sexual assault. These are just some ingredients in Brad Meltzer's epic 7-part comic series titled Identity Crisis. When one of their own is targeted and killed how can the Justice League ensure it will never happen again? Join us as we tackle this controversial series, 20 years after the first issue was published and see whether or not the story was a polarizing as we remember. Do the themes Meltzer manages to bring up hold up? Listen in and find out!

title: Major Issues Ep 334: Fantastic Four (1994) Recap and Review
embedCode:
In this episode of the Major Issues Podcast, in memory of Roger Corman, we dive into the vault of superhero cinema history to unearth the 1994 Fantastic Four film, a movie shrouded in mystery and controversy. Often regarded as the "lost" Fantastic Four film, this unreleased Marvel adaptation has become a cult classic over the years. Join us as we explore the behind-the-scenes drama, the production's shoestring budget, and the film's eventual status as a bootlegged treasure among comic book fans. Was the film as bad as people say? Are there any redeeming qualities? Can you just manhandle a blind woman in broad daylight? Some of these questions and more will be answered in this FANTASTIC episode!

embedCode:
THE FIRST EPISODE UNDER PARTNERSHIP WITH DIRT SHEET RADIO

"Do A Powerbomb" by Daniel Warren Johnson isn't just your typical graphic novel; it's a visceral experience and a love letter to prowrestling that grabs you by the collar, and doesn't let go. In this episode of the Major Issues Podcast, we'll explore the thematic layers woven throughout the narrative. From the thrill of victory to the agony of defeat, Johnson's storytelling prowess shines as he navigates the tumultuous world of professional wrestling. We'll discuss the nuanced characters, the pulse-pounding action sequences, and the emotional beats that resonate long after you've turned the final page.

Whether you're a die-hard fan of wrestling, comic book aficionado, or simply someone who appreciates masterful storytelling, this episode is sure to captivate and inspire. So grab your headphones, settle in, get ready for that hot tag and prepare to be unleashed into the world of "Do A Powerbomb", in celebration of our collaboration with Dirt Sheet Radio!

title: Major Issues Podcast Ep 324: The Ultimates #7-13 Recap and Review
embedCode:
We're back to finish the second part of our Ultimate story and boy is it a doozy.

The Ultimates confront threats such as the Chitauri, an alien race posing a significant danger to Earth. However, their unity is constantly tested due to personal conflicts and power struggles among team members. Issues such as Captain America's distrust of Nick Fury's leadership, Thor's arrogance, and Hank Pym's insecurities and abusive behavior towards his wife, Janet van Dyne (The Wasp), come to the forefront.

The series explores themes of power, responsibility, and the consequences of wielding immense abilities. It also delves into the darker aspects of superheroism, showcasing the flaws and vulnerabilities of its characters. Join us as we discuss these themes and more in this ULTIMATE finale of our 2 part Ultimate review!

title: Major Issues Ep 310: Batman: Mask of The Phantasm - 30 Year Anniversary Review!
embedCode:
Batman: Mask of The Phantasm is 30 years old! Released on December 25, 1993, Phantasm holds the distinction of being the first animated Batman film to be given a theatrical release. Unfortunately, because of the film's scant promotion, it underperformed at the box-office and it would be close to 25 years until an animated Batman graced the silver screen again. Join us as we look back fondly at this film, debate its talking points and just gush over the style of Batman the animated series.

RIPD is another comic book film that fell off a lot of people's radar even though it stars many actors who will eventually bring life to many a comic book role. Join us as we review the film for its 10 Year anniversary, give our first thoughts as none of us has seen it, and talk about the latest news, rumors, and scuttlebutt regarding comic books and comic book media!

title: Major Issues Ep 285: Punisher Warzone 15 Years Later
embedCode:
Punisher Warzone is a one of the few Marvel films that flew under the radar. Released the same year as the MCU began, it was quickly overshadowed and, in some ways, forgotten.  To commemorate the life of Ray Stevenson who brought Frank Castle to life in Warzone, we will be doing a full recap and review 15 years later. Does the film hold up, or is it a reminder of how comic book films go wrong? All that plus the box office for Across The SpiderVerse, Marvel's Spider-Man 2 news And much more in this jammed packed Major Issues Episode!

embedCode:
5 years ago, Sony Animation released a film that changed how we viewed Spider-Man forever when Spider-Man: Into The Spider-Verse was released in theaters around the globe. title: Major Issues Ep 277: I Kill Giants Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Barbara Thorson is many things. She's a daughter and a student. She is a sister and a dungeon master. Most importantly, Barbara Thorson is a GIANT KILLER! title: Major Issues Ep 264: Predicting 2023!
embedCode:

embedCode:
The Major Issues Podcast is on a MAJOR HIATUS! Don't worry. We microwaved this episode for you guys to enjoy and to finish up our Spidey Month!

This was fate. Ever since the first episode of Which WAS Worse concluded, it was fated for Dan The ComicMan and The Don to do battle again. This time the war has one goal. We must finally figure out which is the worst Spider-Man film of all time. (With Dan defending Spider-Man 3 and The Don advocating for Amazing Spider-Man 2) Nothing is off the table as the guys talk odd chemistry, bad haircuts, terrible vilains, and crappy dialogue in a war of words for the ages. embedCode:
The Spidey Month Celebration is coming to a close and this is the last dance. Created by the Dynamic Duo of Jeph Loeb and Time Sale, Spider-Man: Blue is one of the most sentimental comics our friendly neighborhood wall-crawler has been in. It tells the story of how Gwen Stacey and Peter Parker fell in love, as Parker finds himself feeling "blue" on Valentine's Day. Full of heart, humor and heroics, Spider-Man: Blue is a quintessential story in the Spidey lore so join us as we recap and review Spider-Man Blue!

embedCode:
Can you believe it's been 60 years since everyone’s friendly neighborhood spider-man came thwipping into the comic book scene and into our hearts? Hands down one of the greatest superheroes of all time, Spidey has managed to stand the test of time, mostly because of the great foundation that was laid when the character was first introduced in 1962. title: Major Issues Ep 240: Ms Marvel (S1) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
They say it's not the brown girls from New Jersey who become superheroes, but that couldn’t be further from the truth in the MCU! Ms. Marvel has just finished its first season and has officially introduced Kamala Khan to the Marvel Cinematic Universe. Not only is the show unique in its depiction of cultures that were previously unexplored, but it also serves as a coming-of-age story for those of us who grew up not feeling “normal”. The series does some massive changes to Kamala’s lore but are they for the better? Is Marvel’s 6-episode structure working in the long term? Am I the only one who doesn’t like spicy foods? Some of those questions will be answered in our recap and review of Ms. Marvel S1!

title: Major Issues Ep 222: Stray Dogs / Primordial Recap and Review
embedCode:
In this special double-header episode of the Major Issues Podcast, we will be discussing 2 Image Comics books that have everyone talking. First, we tackle Stray Dogs by Tony Fleecs with art by Trish Forstner, which follows Sophie, a ridiculously cute stray who gets brought into a farmhouse full of dogs but things are not what they seem. Secondly, we will discuss Primordial by Jeff Lemire with art by Andrea Sorrentino, which is a bit of Homeward Bound mixed with Interstellar. Take a break from Capes and Cowls and join us in this discussion about pets, what it means to have a home and how love can heal us all.

title: Major Issues Ep 219: Batman - The Long Halloween (Recap and Review)
embedCode:
Batman: The Long Halloween is quite possibly the most influential Batman story ever written, having inspired many adaptions in animation and live-action projects. With The Batman just hours away from a global release, there is no time like the present to review the modern classic that Director Matt Reeves himself stated was a major influence on his film. When a serial killer targeting the mob pops up, only killing people on holidays it is up to Batman, James Gordon and D.A. Harvey Dent to save the soul of Gotham without sacrificing their own in the process. Can Gotham be saved? We'll let you decide in this SUPER DEEP DIVE into The Long Halloween!

embedCode:
When the Flash made its October 7th 2014 debut, the future of the DC television empire under the CW seemed bright! While I wasn't a huge fan of the Scarlet Speedster initially, the show made me a fanatic, with me singing the praises of everything from Team Flash to the menacing Eobard Thawne. title: Major Issues Ep 212: Immortal Hulk Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Since the moment Bruce Banner was exposed to the Gamma rays that transformed him into The Incredible Hulk, the two have been at odds. As the years continued, Bruce, who was diagnosed with Dissociative Identity Disorder, would start to develop other "versions" of the Hulk in his mind. But what happen when one of these versions breaks free? Join us as we discuss all 50 issues of Immortal Hulk by Al Ewing, the critically acclaimed modern classic that acts as a love letter and a step forward for the "Big Guy" and Hulk lore in general. Let us head to The Green Door and review hands down one of the best and most visually provocative comics around, Immortal Hulk!

Welcome back to Blue Valley, the small town in Nebraska protected by a new generation of the JSA led proudly by Courtney Whitmore aka Stargirl! title: Major Issues Ep 204: Y: The Last Man (Season 1) Review
embedCode:
When it was announced that Brian K. Vaughan and  Pia Guerra's dystopian comic Y: The Last Man would be adapted as a television series, many stood with bated breath, anticipating an instant classic. Shortly after debuting on FX, the series was abruptly canceled, shocking all the fans of the Eisner Award-winning book. Why did Y the Last Man get the short end of the stick? What elements were changed for the show and was it for the better? We attempt to answer these questions and more in this conversation about Y: The Last Man Season 1!

title: Major Issues Ep 200: Under The Hood (2006) Comic Review!
embedCode:
Jason Todd the (once canonically) red-headed stepchild of the Batfamily has intrigued readers ever since his return from the dead 15 years ago. Since then he has been used in animation, video games, and has recently made his live-action debut in DC's Titans on HBOMax. title: Major Issues Ep 187: Loki (Season 1) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Loki Laufeyson, the mischievous scamp, and self-appointed God of Mischief did a lot of soul-searching in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. He starts as a villain and eventually becomes an antihero by the time of his death in Avengers Endgame. What would happen if we took a Loki who NEVER found redemption, and threw him into a time-traveling man-hunt for other versions of himself? Then you would have Loki, the Disney+ series about the villain with the same name, as he teams with the Time Variance Authority to defend the Sacred Timeline. What horrors exists in the Void? Who lives at the end of time? Why are alligators so cool? 2/3 of the questions will be answered in this review of Loki Season 1!

embedCode:
Loki Laufeyson is a lot of things, but could he rule the free world? That's the premise of the 2016 4-issue miniseries "Vote Loki", which was written by Christopher Hastings for Marvel Comics. embedCode:
Last we saw Sam Wilson, he was gifted the iconic vibranium shield by Steve Rogers aka Captain America, in hope that he would take up the role and follow the legacy. title: Major Issues Ep 170: Zack Snyder's Justice League (2021) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
The bell has been rung. The fight has been won.
After four years of campaigns, hashtags, and reshoots, Zack Snyder's original vision for the Justice League is here, but is it an improvement on the original? Coming in just a bit over four hours of content, there is a lot to discuss when it comes to an unshackled Snyder in regards to the DCEU. What direction will DC go in now? Should Snyder get another bite of the proverbial apple? When will we #ReleaseTheHotDogCut? Some of those questions and more will be answered as we enter what could be our last time in the Snyderverse.

title: Major Issues Ep 169: House of M (Main Story) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Now that the dust has settled in Westview and with WandaVision in our rearview, now is the perfect time to talk what could quite possibly be the biggest Scarlet Witch story to date; the universe changing House of M written by Brian Michael Bendis and illustrated by Olivier Coipel. The story follows the horrific events of Avengers: Disassembled where The Scarlet Witch with revenge on her mind kills several Avengers using her unpredictable chaos magic. The aftermath saw the disbandment of the Avengers as a team, but not before one last meeting. Fearing the worst of things to come, The X-Men and The Avengers meet to answer a question they never thought they’d have to ask. What do we do about Wanda?

embedCode:
Oh MCU how we have missed you! Just like old friends meeting together for the first time in a long time it turns out we have ALOT to be filled in on when it comes to our favorite Avenging Couple of Wanda Maximoff and the Vision. embedCode:
Perpetua, the creator of the Multiverse with the villainous Batman Who Laughs at her side, has taken over the Multiverse as we know it, plunging it into a dystopian hellscape filled to the brim with nightmare versions of Batman. Our heroes have been split up, some imprisoned, others in hiding and rulers like Wonder Woman and Aquaman have joined Perpetua to save their respective peoples. The year is 2003. You just got finished beating GTA: Vice City and can't wait to download the new Linkin Park new album on Limewire. embedCode:
Ready. Aim. FIRE! We are BACK to finish up our review of Matt Fraction’s Hawkeye, tackling issues 12-22 (& Annual #1) and boy does it get good! title: Major Issues Ep 156: Which WAS Worse VIII - Trial of The Incredible Hulk (1984) Vs Justice League of America (1997)
embedCode:
With the announcement that we will be able to witness Wonder Woman 1984 from the comfort of our own homes via HBO Max, there is no better time to review TWO Straight-To-TV Comic Book Movies from yesteryear. In a WHICH WAS WORSE first, we will be pitting two films that have NEVER had a theatrical release. In one corner is Marvel’s The Trail of The Incredible Hulk from 1985 which features, of all things, a cameo from the Man without Fear himself, Daredevil! In the other corner, the infamous pilot for DC’s Justice League of America from 1994 which was not allowed to use Batman, Superman or Wonder Woman. After a truly abysmal year, join us for some laughs as we try to decide WHICH WAS WORSE!

title: Major Issues Ep 154: Doomsday Clock Review!
embedCode:
Tick. Tock. Tick. Tock. Three years ago DC Comics embarked on an incredibly ambitious task that would see them cross over their main universe with a host of Watchmen characters. A story that was meant to span 1 year, ended up suffering from editorial issues and production woes, but after the dust settles....is it any good? As time marches on in the DC Universe we revisit Doomsday Clock, discuss its themes, and try to guess what writer Geoff Johns was really trying to say. Nothing ever ends but join us as we close THIS chapter in WATCHMEN history!

title: Major Issues EP 144: For The PODCAST That Has Everything (Black Mercy Retrospective)
embedCode:
When Alan Moore first wrote "For The Man Who Has Everything", for Superman Annual #11 he set out to tell a story that explored the Man of Steel's deepest desires. Still considered one of the best single issue stories of all time, the duo behind Watchmen churned out an instant classic that had ripple effects on the comic book medium. Almost 20 years later, the story has been adapted in animation, has had its elements replicated in live-action, and just as recently as the year 2020 has spawned a comic sequel of sorts. Join us as we discuss that Superman issue, its adaptation on both Justice League Unlimited and Supergirl, and the two-part Justice League story Garden of Evil in this ode to all things Black Mercy!

title: Major Issues Ep 143: The Joker 80th Anniversary Comic Review!
embedCode:
Quite possibly the most popular supervillain in comic book history, The Joker has managed to stay relevant and on the tongues of fans for 80 years. During that time he has adapted and evolved as different creative teams do their best to make their version of the Clown Prince of Crime memorable. DC’s has chosen to commemorate those 8 decades of the dastardly degenerate with a 100-page Super Spectacular, giving the best and brightest artists and writers a shot at celebrating the Harlequin of Hate. In this deep look at the Joker we see why he’s managed to stick around so long, and discuss what makes the sick clown tick in this episode of the Major Issues Podcast.

embedCode:
“The Phoenix burns, Galactus devours, the Watchers witness, the Celestials judge. Thanos lives. Thanos denies. Thanos wins.” That is the order of the Marvel Comics UNiverse as we know it, but what if Thanos got what he wanted? What if the undeniable fate of The Mad Titan is truly unavoidable? Thanos Wins (Thanos #13-18) does it's best to answer that question. Written by Donny Cates, Thanos Lives looks at the Marvel Universe millions of years into the future. It is a future in which Thanos has bested all challengers. What happens to a conqueror when there is nothing left to conquer? Tune in and find out!

Even though he was born a normal man, in a normal body Robert Reynolds can't help but think he used to be something SUPER. The trouble is he can't seem to get anyone to believe him. title: Major Issues Ep 133: Agent Carter Complete Series Review!
embedCode:
It is 4th of July weekend 2020, so we thought instead of tackling traditional patriotic heroes in the comic book lexicon, we would take a look at what is quite possibly the most patriotic hero ever. The “Sentinel of Liberty” himself, Captain America is known for his stars and stripes but as important as “The Man Behind The Shield” is, we are here to talk about “The Woman Behind The Man Behind The Shield”. That's right, for this episode of the Major Issues Podcast we will be discussing the entire series of Agent Carter, a show starring Hayley Atwell as longtime Captain America love interest Peggy Carter.

Travel back to the 1940’s after World War II, as we see how Miss Carter deals with the disappearance of Steve Rogers while trying to climb the ranks of the SSR. With cameos by Howard Stark, The Howling Commandos, and Edwin Jarvis himself this show is a must-watch for the completionist comic book tv fan. Join us as we celebrate one of Marvel’s first independent female characters and discuss her journey from page to screen.

embedCode:

title: Major Issues Ep 130: A History of Violence (Film and Book) Review!
embedCode:
Picture it. You're a small town man, living his small town life. You have a beautiful wife, a growing son, and precocious little daughter. You've come to love your little community, and own a soda shop right in the middle of it. You play baseball with neighbors, know the sheriff on a first name basis, and spend most of your time serving members of your hamlet. Then, all of a sudden, dangerous men come into town. INTO YOUR TOWN. They think you're some guy named Joey who did some very bad things in the past, and they are here to get even. What would you do?

That is the plot of A History of Violence, a graphic novel written by John Wagner with Art by Vince Locke. The book was so successful it even had a motion picture adaptation directed by David Cronenberg and starring Viggo Mortensen! Which medium told a better story? Which did the hosts prefer? Let's find out!

title: Major Issues Ep 129: Iron Man: Extremis/Iron Man 3
embedCode:
Although it was a financial success, many saw Iron Man 3 as the weakest of the Tony Stark trilogy. In the search of how the mighty had fallen, we came across the original source material for the film. Written by Warren Ellis and published between 2005 and 2006 Iron Man: Extremis is the modern take on the Tony Stark mythos, and manages to give us just enough backstory to care about the future of the Armored Avenger. It also introduces Extremis, which is a big plot point in the film. So what are the big differences? How does the film stray from its source material? Which is the better story? All this and more will be explored in this arc-reactor powered episode of the Major Issues Podcast!

title: Major Issues Ep 128: Alan Moore & Twilight of Superheroes
embedCode:
Not every story that is pitched to publishers sees the light of day. That's exactly what happened in the late 1980s when, off the success of Watchmen, its writer Alan Moore attempted to pitch a massive DC crossover event titled Twilight of Superheroes. The pitch was denied and the story never saw the light of day, but what if it did? In this in-depth episode we comb through all 33 pages of Alan Moore's pitch which is full to the brim with violence, depression and the derelict heroes that has made the writer famous. Join us as we discuss whether this project was ever worth making, and what impact the comics industry has had on Alan Moore's psyche. It is sure to be one HELLBLAZING episode!

title: Major Issues Ep 126: Wolverine: Old Man Logan Recap and Review
embedCode:
“I'm the best there is at what I do, but what I do best isn't very nice.” That's the famous catchphrase from the clawed and flawed Canadian hero known to the public as Wolverine. From the comics to the big screen, with various forms of animation and adaptation in between, Logan is one of the most popular superheroes of all time. Mark Millar's Wolverine: Old Man Logan takes the character we know and love and puts him in a world we don't understand. A world without X-Men. A world with no Avengers. Most importantly a world where this Logan is a pacifist. Celebrated by many as one of the best Wolverine stories of all time join us as we talk Old Man Logan and its adaptation on the silver screen: Logan (2019) starring Hugh Jackman in his last portrayal of Wolverine!

title: Major Issues Ep 125: Marvel's Runaways #1-18 (S1 & 2) Review!
embedCode:
A wise Will once said "Parents just don't understand." No one feels this sentiment more than the topic of our episode this week Marvel's Runaways! Created in 2003 by Brian K. Vaughan to attract young readers, the Runaways are a group of ragtag teens who discover their parents are evil villains! What do you do when the people you trust are up to no good? You create a superhero team and seek justice! The series was so popular it spawned a series on Hulu! In this episode, we go over the first 18 issues of the hallmark series and dive into the Hulu series for comparison. Grab your dinosaur, take off your inhibiting bracelet and get ready to talk all things Runaways!

title: Major Issues Ep 124: Young Justice (S1-3) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Every generation has its landmark cartoon series. In the late 2000s a concept for merging the popular Teen Titans and Young Justice comics into one show emerged. That show would be called Young Justice and it would change how people viewed the DC Universe forever. We spent years begging for a serious interpretation to our modern-day folklore and we were given a team of angsty young heroes, hell-bent on proving they have what it takes to change the status quo. With tons betrayal, love, identity and heroics, Young Justice was ahead of its time. Join us as we go through all three seasons of this amazing show!

title: Major Issues Ep 123: "What's So Funny Bout Truth, Justice & The American Way?" Recap and Review!
embedCode:
The world has become a very unpredictable place. With misinformation at every turn, citizens are starting to lose hope in the things they always stood for, wondering if the old way of life has become too "old fashioned". Seems like that was also the case in early 2001 in the offices of DC Comics as they wondered if their first hero Superman, needed to change with the times. That brings us Action Comics #775 where a question is asked; What's So Funny Bout Truth Justice & The American Way? In the issue, Superman meets The Elite, a no-nonsense teel of vigilantes that challenge his code of Ethics. Will the man of Steel be swayed? Tune into this review and find out!

The character of Robin aka Dick Grayson, who first graced comic books in 1940, is officially 80 years old! To celebrate, DC put out a Robin 80th Anniversary 100 Page Super Spectacular! GLOW (or Gorgeous Ladies of Wrestling) was a women's professional wrestling promotion that began in 1986 and took a group of models, actresses and soap stars and turned them into larger than life personalities! title: Major Issues Ep 116: Superman: Red Son (2020) Review!
embedCode:
In the far distant past of 2003, comic book author Mark Millar penned a three-issue prestige format comic book mini-series that dared the to ask "What if Superman didn't land in the great ol' US of A?". Suddenly we had Superman: Red Son, an Elseworlds story that deals with the ramifications of a Soviet Superman, a Government-sponsored Lex Luthor and a Bat-terrorist hell-bent on setting the system back with his explosive methods. The story itself is great, and was adapted into animation for the DC Universe app. Join us as we discuss the similarities, differences, high and lows of the Superman: Red Son animated film!

title: Major Issues 115: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003) Review!
embedCode:
What do you get when you cross a vampire, a colonist, an invisible man, a shape-shifting monster and a pirate? Why you get the League of Extraordinary Gentlemen! The comic written co-created in the late 90's by the prolific Alan Moore centered around some of literature's greatest figures as they band together to stop one of histories greatest villains in a plot to take over the world. The 2003 adaptation, which has the strange distinction of being Sean Connery's last film, has some of those moments, and add a bunch of new ones, but does it hold up to the original? title: Major Issues 114: The Alcoholic By Jonathan Ames (Vertigo Comics)
embedCode:
Can you remember the first time you had a drink? How it made you feel? What it made you do? Some look back fondly at how alcohol fueled their younger years but The Alcoholic by Jonathan Ames is no such story. Follow narrator Jonathan A. (no relation) as he takes you through a a collection of memories that has led him to the self-loathing, binge-drinking present where the series starts. This story is not for the weak at heart and deals with sexuality, trauma, death, drugs and more in this true-to-life tale of what happens when you don't tend to old wounds. For anyone seeking help for their own alcohol addiction please call : 1-800-662-HELP (4357) or visit https://www.samhsa.gov/.

title: Major Issues Ep 112: Harley Quinn Retrospective
embedCode:
There has never been another person more synonymous with The Joker than Harley Quinn. Seduced while trying to “save” the clown prince of crime, the former doctor’s decent into madness is one of comic’s most tragic tales, but is not the end of the Harley Quinn Story. Join us as we trace back through the life that is Harley’s, from her start in animation, to her evolution in video games, comics, and other media. We also review the DC Black Label series Harleen and stack her latest origin story against her Mad Love beginnings (Batman The Animated Series) and her New 52 Origin. All of this in preparation of our Birds of Prey review next week!

title: Major Issues Ep 111: Batman: White Knight by Sean Murphy Review
embedCode:
The war between Batman and The Joker has always had Gotham trapped in its wake, with the GCPD usually standing by helpless. Written and Drawn by Sean Murphy and released under the DC Black Label imprint, Batman White Knight turns everything we know about Batman and Joker’s relationship on its head in a story that by asking us,  is Batman really what’s best for Gotham or Can the Joker ever really be redeemed?  Most stories involve Joker trying his best to break The Bat, and The Dark Knight doing his best not to execute the crazed clown killer, but this one takes a more modern approach.  Join us in a story complete with twists and turns, that provide a sharp social commentary on vigilantism but also an in-depth look at a semi-sane Joker aka Jack Napier.

title: Major Issues Ep 110: The War of The Realms & Jason Aaron's Thor Talk!
embedCode:
Malekith the Dark Elf has only ever known War. He is an instrument of its will and for years he has been threatening the Realms with violence and malicious intentions. Since early on in Jason Aaron's run on Thor, Malekith has spoken about a war to involve all the realms and its inhabitants and it is finally here! The War of the Realms is upon us, as Malekith brings his army to Midgard (Earth) for an unprecedented conflict of epic proportions. Join Thor, The Avengers, Spider-Man and more as they try and take the Dark Elf down once and for all, and join us at Major Issues as we gush over what Jason Aaron was able to do with the Thor character!

title: Ep 109: Crisis on Infinite Earths (Arrowverse) Recap and Review
embedCode:
CW'S most ambitious crossover has finally ended after 5 heart-stopping episodes! Taking it's story from the iconic Crisis on Infinite Earths comic, the "Arrowverse" surely had some big shoes to fill, in trying to adapt the classic tale. Face to face with the extinction-level event, the Monitor gathers Green Arrow, the Flash, Supergirl, Batwoman, Sara Lance, Ray Palmer, and several "Paragons" from throughout the multiverse to stop the Anti-Monitor from destroying all of reality. Did our heroes manage to save the multiverse from an untimely end, or did the might of the Anti-Matter Wave wipe out existence as we know it? That plus cameos, easter eggs and laughs in what can affectionately be called the biggest love-letter to DC Media ever created!

title: Major Issues Ep 104: Crisis on Infinite Earths (1986) Comic Review!
embedCode:
Worlds will LIVE! Worlds will DIE! Nothing will ever BE THE SAME! Those are the words spoken by the character Pariah as he watched universe upon universe being destroyed by Antimatter energy. As the multiverse collapses, its up to the heroes of DC Comics to turn the tide against a villainous foe, in one of the first crossovers of its kind Crisis on Infinite Earths. Featuring character from DC's past and present Crisis is vital reading if you want to understand the current state of the DC universe, and helps us gear up for the MASSIVE Crisis on Infinite Earths CW Crossover! Join us on this trip throughout the multiverse and through time as we cover one of the biggest stories DC has ever done!

title: Major Issues Ep 103: Kingdom Come (1996) Comic Review!
embedCode:
The internet basically EXPLODED when it was revealed that Superman Returns actor Brandon Routh will be bringing the KINGDOM COME Superman to life in Crisis on Infinite Earths, as it should. Not only is Kingdom Come one of DC's best Elseworlds stories, but it acts as a love letter to almost every decade of DC, while providing some commentary on the ever growing violence in today's comics. In the center of this is Kingdom Come's Superman, a relic of days gone who may be the key to changing society forever. Considered by many the quintessential DC graphic novel, we dive back in to Kingdom Come almost 25 years later, and see if its morals and lessons still hold up! What does Kingdom Come say about superheroes as a whole? You'll have to listen to find out!

embedCode:
Deep in the sewers of New York City lives a fearsome fighting team with incredible ninja skills, and an affinity for pizza. title: Major Issues Ep 101: Superman Vs Terminator: Death to the Future
embedCode:
With the release of Terminator Dark fate, the guys at ComicBook Clique look through the archives and review the 2000 comic Superman Vs Terminator: Death to the Future. Filled with easter eggs from both Dc Comics and the Terminator franchise, the comic itself is the erfect encapsulation of late 90’s early 2000s comics, where its most all style and little substance. Nearly 20 years since its initial printing, how did the story hold up? Why is the Terminator franchise having such a hard time bouncing back from T2? How the heck does Supergirl have telepathic powers? This and many other random questions will be answered in this sci-fi episode of the Major Issues Podcast!

title: Major Issues Ep 98: Tales From DC Black Label I- Joker/Batman Damned!
embedCode:
It's the spookiest time of the year, which is the perfect time for some TALES FROM DC BLACK LABEL! Created in 2018, DC Black Label was intended for more mature readers and is filled with unapologetically dark material, starring some of DC's well known characters. In our first adventure Joker (written in 2008 by Brian Azzarello w art by Lee Bermejo) we follow henchmen Jonny Frost as he chuefers the clown prince of crime around Gotham. In Batman: Damned mysterious forces are trying to hide the truth from Batman, but can he get to the bottom of it with the help of John Constantine? Join us as we review these twisted tales from DC's Black Label!

embedCode:
Look up in the sky! It's a bird! It's a plane! It's Supergirl's debut on the silver screen!
Supergirl (1984) stars Helen Slater, Faye Dunaway, and Peter O'Toole and is seemingly ignored when talking about the pantheon on films based on DC Comics characters. title: Major Issues Ep 96: Joker (2019) Recap & Review!
embedCode:
No comic book movie has proven to be more polarizing lately than 2019's Joker, directed by Todd Phillips and starring Joaquin Phoenix. While some praise the gritty look at Gotham's clown prince of crime, others fear the repercussions of making a homicidal madman the hero of the story. Good thing your friends at the Major Issues Podcast are hear to weigh on all things Joker in this in-depth recap and review. In it we go scene by scene and dissect what makes this film both unique and important, whilst also discussing what films it pays homage to. It's time to grab some face paint, dance like noone's watching and put on a happy face as we tackle a new age of DC film by reviewing Joker.

title: Major Issues Ep 95: Arrow (CW) Retrospective!
embedCode:
It's been nearly a decade since billionaire playboy Oliver Queen was rescued from purgatory on CW, and decided to avenge his father's death by cleaning up Starling City as The Hood. Since then the Emerald Archer has had to deal with shadow organizations, super soldiers, magic totems, resurrections, doomsday plots, leagues of assassins, and more double crosses than one can fire an arrow at. In the same breath, the Green Arrow has made some friends in high places, and has been a part of some of the greatest comic book crossovers we've ever seen. Join us as we take a look back at 7 seasons of action, adventure and archery, as we celebrate the show that kicked off the Arrowverse, Arrow!

title: Major Issues Ep 94: Spider-Man Life Story by Chip Zdarsky Recap & Review!
embedCode:
In 1962, in AMAZING FANTASY #15, 15-year-old Peter Parker was bitten by a radioactive spider and became the Amazing Spider-Man! Fifty-seven years have passed in the real world since that event — so what would have happened if the same amount of time passed for Peter as well?

A special high-end limited series that’s a part of the celebration of Marvel’s 80th anniversary, SPIDER-MAN: LIFE STORY combines the talents of Chip Zdarsky (SPECTACULAR SPIDER-MAN, MARVEL 2-IN-ONE) and Mark Bagley (AMAZING SPIDER-MAN, ULTIMATE SPIDER-MAN) to tell the entire history of Spider-Man from beginning to end, set against the key events of the decades through which he lived! Join us as we review what is soon to be a cult classic, and follow our favorite web-head through the decades!

title: Major Issues Ep 92: The Boys (Comic and Season 1) Review!
embedCode:
The Boys has hit Amazon Prime Video by storm, if that storm were filled with blood, guts, bones and organs! Starring Karl Urban as Billy Butcher and based on the comic created by Garth Ennis under The Wildstorm imprint, The Boys is set in a universe where superpowered people are recognized as heroes by the general public and owned by powerful corporation Vought International, which ensures that they are aggressively marketed and monetized. These "people of virtue" are less than moral, as they create tons of collateral damage and live in complete excess. These ain't your grandma's superheroes! It's up to Billy and his band of misfits in The Boys to put these "supes" back their place, whether they like it or not.

title: Major Issues Ep 91: Batman The Animated Series
embedCode:
Few animated series have stood the test of time the way Batman the Animated Series has. Not only has it redefined a generation of storytelling in animation, but it has expanded upon Batman’s legendary mythos, and has even added some wrinkles to his infamous rouges gallery. Even decades after its release, people still talk about the style, score, and frankly adult like content in a medium that was daring itself to evolve. Join us as we talk all the things that made Batman TAS stand out in the golden age of carton television, marvel over the sheer talent of the voice acting, and reminisce over some of the best episodes the show had to offer, in this nostalgic look at a hallmark in The Dark Knight’s lore!

title: Major Issues Ep 87: Mister Miracle By Tom King (2018) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Scott Free has escaped a lot of things in his life, like the firepits of Apokolips and multiple near death experiences facing off to defend New Genesis. This time Eisner Award Winning Writer Tom King puts Scott aka Mister Miracle in a situation he can’t just escape from, and makes him face the trauma that shaped him head on. Mister Miracle, as a story is a brilliant exploration into the psyche of war, fatherhood and heroics in the face of insurmountable odds. Mister Miracle as a person is all of us, trying to navigate our memories and emotions and come out the other end unscathed. Listen now for our uncensored thoughts on this modern day classic.

title: Major Issues Ep 86: Batman: Hush (2019) Recap & Review!
embedCode:
When one of Batman’s most famous stories gets an animated adaptation, you KNOW Major Issues is on the case! Based on the Jeph Loeb and Jim Lee story of the same name written in 2003, Batman Hush takes Bruce Wayne back to his detective roots, and does its best to honor the source material while offering fresh surprises for those who follow the comics.

There is still the Catwoman /Batman action the story is famous for, with some added twists and turns along the way. In the film, Batman’s enemies are acting stranger than usual, and a mysterious bandaged puppet-master seems to be at the center of the chaos. Featuring cameos from the greatest rogue gallery in comic book history, a love affair between masked vigilantes, and some painful blasts from the past, can the World’s Greatest Detective solve this increasingly personal puzzle and find love?

title: Major Issues Ep 84: PTSD and Superheroes (Heroes in Crisis Review)
embedCode:
What happens when you mix the best and brightest superheroes in DC’s pantheon with the very real ramifications of dealing trauma and mental health? You get Tom King’s 9 issue limited series entitled Heroes in Crisis. King, a former CIA agent in his own right is no stranger to subjects like PTSD, but how does his brand of storytelling gel with the rest of the DC Universe?

Follow along as we talk about Sanctuary, a safe haven for all heroes dealing with trauma. When multiple heroes turn up dead, it’s up to the Justice League to find the culprit in a story that tries to show us a different side of our favorite caped characters. Did King’s message come through? What does HIC say about mental health? We talk all that and more in our review of Heroes in Crisis!

The second installment of Holland’s web-slinging hero brings back the heart, humor, and spectacle of the first whilst turning the knob on the stakes way up. Now that MCU's Spider-Man has swung back into theaters and into our hearts, was the film any good?

FFH follows Tom Holland aka Peter Parker tries to navigate life post Avengers:Endgame. For the first time since his introduction, Spider-Man seems to not want the responsibility placed on him as the “next Tony Stark”, he just wants to be a regular kid. title: Major Issues Ep 78: The Dark Phoenix Saga (1980) Comic Review!
embedCode:
Arguably the most adapted comic book story ever, The Dark Phoenix Saga is story of absolute power corrupting absolutely. Written years before such classics like Watchmen, Secret Wars, & The Dark Knight Returns, the Saga itself is emotional, daring, and the quintessential X-Men Story. From being adapted in X-Men The Last Stand, to its animated interpretation in the 90’s it was important to go back to the actual comic issues that made all of those worth it. Join us as Chris Claremont and John Byrne read us the most traumatic bedtime story ever, the always exceptional Dark Phoenix Saga.

title: Major Issues Ep 77: X-Ploring The X-Men Movie Franchise!
embedCode:
A decade before the Marvel Cinematic Universe was a thing, 20th Century Fox had an idea. They wanted to introduce the characters they purchased from Marvel Comics, The X-Men on the big screen. Come the year 2000, they achieved their goal and that first X-Men film’s popularity would eventually let Fox create an entire franchise of interconnected films in the X-Men Universe. Sadly with the release of Dark Phoenix we will be seeing the end of these interconnected films, so it’s time to put on the rose colored glasses, look into the rear-view and X-Plore the X-Men Film Franchise!

In this episode we go over our favorite moments, characters, and costumes in the franchise, while also looking it it’s not-so-proud moments…(looking at you Origins). It is nostalgia fueled thrill-ride with your friends at ComicBook Clique, so hang on and get ready, cause The Clique’s Gonna Give It To YA!

title: Major Issues Ep 76: Cosmic Ghost Rider Vol.1: Baby Thanos Must Die (2018)
embedCode:
When an alternate universe Frank Castle was on Death’s door after a battle with Thanos, he had one wish.... “I would give ANYTHING to PUNISH that purple Son of a B*%$#”. That was all the demonic Mephisto needed to hear to make a deal with Frank and make him the Ghost Rider aka the Spirit of Vengeance. Since then it has been quite a long journey for Castle, as he became the herald of Galactus, was embued with the power cosmic, and became the Black Right Hand of The Mad King.

Fast forward to his defeat by the hands of the Silver Surfer, and you have the beginning of Cosmic Ghost Rider Vol. 1 Baby Thanos Must Die. This 5 issue miniseries written by Donny Cates with art by Dylan Burnett is beautifully insane in its concepts and tragically amusing with its premise as a time traveling Frank Castle tries to murder Baby Thanos before he can’t grow up to be the Mad Titan and worse, The Mad King.

title: Major Issues Ep 75: The Umbrella Academy (2019) Review
embedCode:
On October 1, 1989, 43 women around the world give birth simultaneously, despite none of them showing any sign of pregnancy until labor began. Seven of the children are adopted by eccentric billionaire Sir Reginald Hargreeves, and turned into a superhero team through what he calls "The Umbrella Academy". On February 15, 2019 Netflix debuted an adaptation of the comic book series of the same name, created by Gerard Way and Gabriel Bá, and on May 15 2019, the guys at Major Issues review that very show! 

From daddy issues, to dealing with trauma, the Umbrella Academy is a masterclass in what dysfunctional family should be. Join us as we pick our favorite member, go over our favorite scenes and rock out to the awesome soundtrack in this in-depth review!

title: Major Issues Ep 64: Which Was Worse III (Catwoman 2004  VS Elektra 2005)
embedCode:
Captain Marvel is on the cusp of breaking ground for the Marvel Cinematic Universe as its first big-budget attempt at a female-led superhero film, adding a layer to the genre that was sorely needed. We've seen how great films like Wonder Woman can inspire a generation so here's hoping Captain Marvel follows suit, but that's not what THIS episode is about. One THIS episode we bring back a classic segment, "Which Was Worse" where we pit two maligned films against one another in a debate over which is truly the WORST film.

With Captain Marvel around the corner we've chosen 2004's Catwoman and 2005's Elektra as the films to go head to head. Nearly 15 years later, many tout these films as everything wrong with the superhero genre, but are both TRULY that bad? Is Catwoman definitively worse than Elektra or is the Scarlet Assassin the reason why it took us so long to get a competent female led superhero film? Tune in to find out!

title: Major Issues Ep 63: The Multiple Magnificent Mishaps of Jamie "Multiple Man" Madrox
embedCode:
Character Spotlight Time! This week the Don talks to Jason Estrella, cosplayer, comic reader and self professed Multiple Man fanatic! The two talk about the changling Jamie Madrox aka Multiple Man but why the spotlight on Ol' Madrox anyway? Well the character has been through some ups and downs over the decades and was even considered as the star of his own movie starring James Franco! He's made cameos in movies and video games and has even headed his own version of X-Factor. 

Jamie is an interesting hero. He seems to not care and yet always goes out of his way to do good. He may not be as popular as his other X-Mates but that ends with this episode! Starting with his debut in Giant-Sized Fantastic Four #4 and leaded up to his most recent appearance in the 2018 Multiple Man miniseries we try to cover the multiple magnificent mishaps of Jamie "Multiple Man" Madrox!

title: Major Issues Ep 62: Scott Pilgrim Vs The World (2010) Recap & Review
embedCode:
HERE COMES A NEW CHALLEN- uh PODCAST! Blow off that cartridge, plug in your vintage controller, and get ready for a hell of a ride as we look back at 2010's Scott Pilgrim Vs The World, directed by (almost Ant-Man) director Edgar Wright. The film has a tremendous cast, which serves as a who's who of comic book media actors, while the stunning visual effects make sure you are never bored. It is also extremely accurate to its source material, the Scott Pilgrim series written by Bryan Lee O'Malley.

Once we get over the special effects, sick fight sequences, and hilarious "ripped from the pages" dialogue, we talk about the themes this movie presents, and whether or not we are supposed to like its quirky protagonist, Scott Pilgrim himself! We discuss why this movie was a financial failure, why it has such a cult following, chat about or favorite fight scenes, and do apt comparisons to the comic. Last but not least we talk in-depth about the film's alternate ending, why it does (or doesn't) work and who the TRUE HERO of this film is!

title: Major Issues Ep 61: Marvel's The Punisher (Character Analysis)
embedCode:
One Batch, Two Batch, Penny and Dime. The Punisher is a character that has transcended the pages of modern comics and has come into his own as a enigmatic anti-hero. While no portrayal is 100% accurate, Jon Bernthal's ability to transform himself into the One-Man-War on crime in DareDevil Season 2 and then his own show The Punisher on Netflix, most are ready to call him the best Punisher ever! On this episode we analyze the character of Frank Castle as depicted across the Marvel Universe on Netflix, starting with his dynamic debut against the Devil of Hell Kitchen, and ending with his final war cry as his series seemingly ends.

We talk the effects of PTSD on mental health, whether revenge could ever make someone whole, and try to walk in Frank's shoes as he chooses to fight for good while thinking he deserves none of it himself. We also spoil the heck out Season 1 & 2 of The Punisher, (and some Daredevil) so make sure you've done your homework before listening to our version of Frank Castle 101 courtesy of your friends at ComicBook Clique!

title: Major Issues Ep 60: The Vision (2016) by Tom King Review
embedCode:
The Don is not completely digging Tom King's Batman run, and thought it would be a good idea to read some of King's earlier work, more specifically his critically acclaimed run on The Vision in 2016. Boy, Oh Boy...where to start with this one? From deep themes of humanity and sentience, to nuanced views on mental health and what it means to be normal, Tom King's 12 issue (two volume) run on The Vision is a bonafide master class in deconstructing the superhero genre. With Yogi, as his co-host, The Don hopes to unpack the metric ton of thematic elements that is this book! We cannot stress enough how good this story is, so do us a favor a pick it up so you can join the conversation!
Comixology : https://buff.ly/2WivZ0C Amazon: https://buff.ly/2WmyYp9

title: Major Issues Ep 58: DC's Titans Review
embedCode:
The first season of DC Universe’s inaugural show is over, and when the dust settled how did we feel about "DC’s Titans"? This week The Don is joined by GT Rebirth and Yogi for a no hold barred discussion reviewing the first live action iteration of one of the most famous teams in comic; The (Teen) Titans. From Beast Boy to Robin, Raven to Starfire and with a mix of Hawk and Dove, DC didn’t pull any punches in this series. Take a deep dive as we talk history of the team, comic book continuity and marvel at the very adult situations tackled on this show. Did it live up to expectations? Was Kory’s outfit that bad? Did Dick Grayson’s butt get its due? Most of those questions and more will be answered on this week Major Issues Podcast!

title: Major Issues Ep 57: Worst Comic Book Movie Sequels of all Time (w/ Peter Melnick)
embedCode:
An airtight sequel is hard to come by these days. It seems like every studio under the sun NEEDS an interconnecting universe of films now. This means sometimes what could once stand alone as a fantastic first outing for a franchise, ultimately gets bogged down by unnecessary sequels. Comic book franchises have these issues in spades, as fundamental misunderstandings of what made the first film work, often leads to very very very bad sequels.

From disco dancing down the street to peeing fire out the wazoo, these missteps in comic book media exist, and on this episode of Major Issues we do our best to cover the Worst Comic Book Movie Sequels of All Time. Joining The Don this week is comic book media commentator and podcast aficionado Peter Melnick of The Marvelist podcast. Do they have the same parameters for what makes a bad sequel? Listen now and find out!

title: Major Issues Ep 56: Spider-Man: Into The Spider-Verse (2018) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
It’s the first show of the 2019 reviewing one of the last comic book movies of 2018. (Trust us; we left this one for last for a reason!) On this episode of Major Issues, the guys of Comic Book Clique discuss Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse. With the recent increase in Spider-Man’s popularity between the PS4 game, his inclusion in Infinity War and the Spider-geddon comic arc, you would think most, if not all of us, would be sick of the web shooting, wall crawling superhero by now!

I mean how many times can one hear about the spider bite that turned mild mannered Peter Parker into the amazing Spider-man we know and love? How can you redefine a timeless story for a new audience and better yet a new demographic? Spider-Verse does it and does it seamlessly. Just when you think you’ve seen it all, this gem of a film will have you gasping, laughing, crying and cheering and that’s in the first half hour. Wanna hear more? Check out this SUPER SPOILER filled review and discussion of all things “Into the Spider-Verse"!

title: Major Issues Ep 54: Aquaman (2018) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
It’s the bottom of the 9th, with bases loaded. DC Comics and Warner Bros are down 5-0 in 2018 with no live action films since Justice League (Nov 2017). The mysterious Aquaman is at bat and the whole world watches in a hushed silence, wondering if director James Wan’s epic tale of royalty and destiny can get DC on the board, and ultimately turn the tide for the studio as a whole.

But…did Aquaman change how fans feel about the DCEU? Is Jason Momoa the whiskey drinking, trident wielding, hero we never knew we needed? Where does one wash their clothes in an underwater city? Some of the questions (and MORE) will be answered on this week’s spoiler filled episode, where we take a deep dive into 2018's Aquaman!

title: Major Issues Ep 53: Avengers Endgame Trailer/ CW Elseworlds Crossover
embedCode:
It's the trailer everyone has been waiting for! The aftermath of The Decimation has left our MCU heroes in a bit of slump as they try to live in a world Post-Snappening. With minimal footage and even less visible plot threads to pull, see what we think of the Avengers: Endgame trailer and put on your tinfoil hat as we speculate wildly! Plus...

Red skies, alternate realities and devilish doppelgangers means we got another Crisis on our hands as CW gives us another epic crossover event with Elseworlds! Starting on The Flash and continuing through Arrow and Supergirl, Elseworlds is a hefty helping of fan service with a metric ton of comic book Easter Eggs and nods at DCs past. All this plus the debut of Gotham city and its resident vigilante Batwoman! Twist, turns, and ominous warnings are on the menu in this feast that just keeps on giving!

title: Major Issues Ep 52: V for Vendetta (2005) Recap & Review
embedCode:
Remember, remember....the fifth of November. The Gunpowder treason and plot. 
I know of no reason why the Gunpowder treason should ever be forgot! These are the first lines spoken in 2005's V for Vendetta based on the book of the same name written by prolific comic book writer Alan Moore. V for Vendetta is a thrill ride of vigilantism, with twists, turns, poetry and violence. Does it hold up almost 15 years later? Does the central plot reflect modern politics? What is the difference between the book and the film? All these questions and more are answered in this week's V for Vendetta Review!

title: Major Issues Ep 49: Kick-Ass (2010) Recap and Review!
embedCode:
Being a hero may not be what it’s cracked up to be. At least that’s the lesson learned in the 2010 smash-hit Kick-Ass based on the 2008 comic of the same name. Kick-Ass follows Dave Lizewski (Aaron Taylor Johnson), a normal New York teenager, as he tries his hand at vigilantism, with some discouraging results. His journey puts him in the path of other heroes (Nicholas Cage & Chloe Grace Moretz), and villains (Mark Strong & Christopher Mintz-Plasse), but things definitely don’t go the way he planned. Directed by Matthew Vaughn with the comic written by Mark Millar, Kick-Ass was a tour de force when it hit theaters and bookshelves…but does it hold up? Lucky for you listeners the guys at CBC decided to review BOTH the film and the comic, in an in-depth review you do NOT wanna miss!

title: Major Issues Ep 44: Venom Lethal Protector (1993) Recap and Review
embedCode:
Its our FINAL STOP on the road to the highly anticipated Venom film due out October 5th! To prepare for the movie, the guys at CBC decided to look at some of the source material used for the production, specifically Venom's first story arc Venom: Lethal Protector. Considered required reading when you think of the character, Lethal Protector follows Eddie Brock as he ditches the NYC Skyline for San Francisco, hoping to become a hero himself. Instead he is met with a slew of bad guys, shifty businessmen and the Life Foundation, a group of rich people arming themselves for the apocalypse. Its one wild ride but shows how great the symbiotic relationship between Brock and Venom truly is!

title: Major Issues Ep 43: Spider-Man The Animated Series - The Venom Saga!
embedCode:
With the Venom film moving ever closer, the guys get nostalgic and get back in the Delorean. The destination? 1994! Back when things were all that and a bag of chips, one of the most prolific cartoons based on a comic was born, with the debut of Spider-Man the Animated Series! The show educated an entire generation of wall crawling Spidey fans, and it was on THIS show that many got their first look at Venom and the alien suit in the  part story The Alien Costume. How does the story/show hold up close to 25 years later? You gotta listen to find out.

title: Major Issues Ep 42: Iron Fist Season 2 Review
embedCode:
Iron Fist Season 2 is here and it's...good? Things are a bit crazy this time around in Chinatown, as we follow a Danny Rand (Finn Jones) aka The Immortal Iron Fist, as he keeps Matt Murdock's promise to keep the city safe. He is joined by the incredibly charming Colleen Wing (Jessica Henwick), the trouble-ridden Meechums, and a shady figure from his past hell bent on revenge. Is Danny Rand a better character this time around? How does this season stack up against its predecessor? What do we hope for the future of the franchise? Check out our full spoiler-filled review of all things Iron Fist Season 2.

title: Major Issues Ep 39: The Bill Finger Fiasco/ Batman & Bill Review
embedCode:
Imagine co-creating one of the greatest superheroes of all time, and never getting credit for it. Imagine designing his gear, building his rouge's gallery and even writing his origin, only to have someone else take the credit for decades. Such was the case of Bill Finger, the man who co-created Batman in 1939, only to have his business partner Bob Kane take sole credit. Bob became and celebrity and Bill? Well you have to listen to our review of the Hulu documentary "Batman & Bill" which details the Bill Finger Fiasco in a way that is full of twists, turns, and even a happy ending!

title: Major Issues Ep 32: Ant-Man & The Wasp (2018) Review
embedCode:
The final film of Phase 3 is in the books as the Marvel Cinematic Universe looks to the future with another great addition in Ant-Man & The Wasp, Starring Pual Rudd, Evangaline Lilly, Michael Douglass & Michelle Pfeiffer. With new villains like Ghost (played by Hanna John-Kamen), old allies like Bill Foster (played by Laurence Fishburne), and the return of the GREAT Michael Pena as Luis, there are tons of things to like about this film. Does it give us all the answer we need? Well..yes and no. No film is perfect and the fact that this one comes off the heels of Infinity War, that didnt do it any favors. Still the cast shines and the true greatness is in the details regardless of how small.
